real_architecture_summary: Reliability centers on CLI smoke generation, artifact/schema validators, manifest checks, and smoke data builders. Synthetic-truth detector scoring in CI is mostly posthoc report-window validation, while the heavier detector-execution harness lives under project/tests/synthetic_truth/* and is not strongly represented in PR CI.
top_invariants: Public smoke CLI flags must actually propagate into the executed smoke run and summary artifacts.
Synthetic-truth validation should prove detector inference, not just artifact presence and timestamp overlap.
PR CI should gate the highest-risk deterministic surfaces before merge.

findings.title: CLI smoke entrypoint silently ignores --seed and --storage_mode
findings.evidence: cli_smoke.main() parses seed and storage_mode, but calls run_smoke_cli() without passing either argument. Direct execution leaves environment.storage_mode at parquet even when csv-fallback is requested.
findings.why_it_matters: The maintained smoke CLI does not honor its own operator-facing contract, and tier2 only exercises defaults.
findings.suggested_remediation: Pass the parsed args into run_smoke_cli() and add a CLI-level contract test.

findings.title: Synthetic-truth validation in CI is mostly artifact-plumbing validation, not full inference-chain validation
findings.evidence: validate_synthetic_detector_truth.py reads precomputed reports and scores them; it never invokes detector code. The golden workflow smoke test fakes the pipeline runner and monkeypatches the validator itself.
findings.why_it_matters: A stale or fabricated report can satisfy CI without proving detector inference worked on the synthetic input.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Inspect validate_synthetic_detector_truth.py and test_golden_synthetic_discovery.py side by side.
findings.suggested_remediation: Add one deterministic end-to-end canary that runs the real detector path and validates the real emitted report without monkeypatching.

findings.title: PR CI tiers do not gate several high-risk deterministic surfaces before merge
findings.evidence: Only tier1 runs on pull_request, and it does not include smoke workflows, synthetic-truth canaries, or broad PIT coverage. tier2 is main-only and tier3 is nightly/release/manual.
findings.why_it_matters: Breakage in smoke orchestration, synthetic-truth wiring, and PIT invariants can merge without PR-time detection.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Compare workflow triggers and invoked tests across tier1/tier2/tier3 plus Makefile minimum-green-gate.
findings.suggested_remediation: Promote a minimal subset of tier2 into PR gating: CLI smoke, one synthetic-truth canary, and one PIT canary.

findings.title: PIT and promotion safety helper modules exist but are entirely outside test and CI coverage
findings.evidence: repo search finds definitions for promotion_gate / verify_pit_integrity / temporal_lint, but no tests or workflow invocations.
findings.why_it_matters: Either critical PIT/promotion safety guards are unverified, or dead code creates a false sense of protection.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Search project/tests and .github/workflows for promotion_gate and temporal_lint references.
findings.suggested_remediation: Add narrow deterministic unit tests or remove/deprecate the unused safety-helper surfaces.

findings.title: Promotion smoke test contains stale defect commentary that contradicts current runtime behavior
findings.evidence: The test comment still claims run_smoke_cli("promotion") is broken, but runtime full-mode smoke now validates promotion artifacts successfully.
findings.why_it_matters: Stale defect commentary distorts audit and maintenance work even when runtime behavior has improved.
findings.reproduction_or_validation: Run run_smoke_cli("full") and compare the resulting promotion summary with the stale comment.
findings.suggested_remediation: Remove/update the stale comment and add an explicit promotion-mode smoke test.

test_coverage_assessment: Artifact-shape and selected deterministic smoke paths are reasonably covered, but CLI passthrough, PIT helper modules, and true synthetic-truth inference are weakly protected, especially at PR time.
docs_specs_code_contradictions: Makefile minimum-green-gate advertises a stronger baseline than any PR workflow actually runs.
suggested_regression_tests: A CLI smoke passthrough test for --storage_mode and --seed.
open_questions_or_unknowns: Whether tier2 is intentionally excluded from pull_request for cost/runtime reasons or just workflow drift.
